narrative: Shortly after noon, a water main breaks under 1st Avenue North near the intersection with Broadway, sending a geyser of cold water across the asphalt and forcing the city to close the block. The sound carries — a deep thud and then hissing — and within twenty minutes orange cones are up and a DPW crew is rerouting foot traffic. Several Downtown businesses lose water pressure for the afternoon. Jolene, walking back from a nearby deli with a sandwich, has to cross to the other side of the street and passes directly in front of Tuck's Books. She glances in the window. Jolene, detoured by the water main closure, walks past the bookstore window and sees Amber inside — a woman she doesn't know yet, alone behind the counter eating from a paper bag, a stack of books at her elbow. Something about it draws her in. She pushes through the door. They exchange a few words about the chaos on the block; Amber shrugs and says the break happened twice last winter too. Jolene buys a novel she half-recognizes from a review she read months ago. They don't make plans, but there's an ease to the exchange — the kind that makes you think about going back.
events: Water main break on 1st Ave N forces Jolene's detour past Tuck's Books
First encounter between Jolene Voss and Amber Tuck
